name: go-token-bucket-ratelimit
description: A standard-library-only token-bucket rate limiter (concurrency-safe Bucket with injectable clock, capacity + refill-rate, Allow/AllowN/Tokens) plus an HTTP Limiter that maintains one bucket per client IP and provides middleware that responds 429 Too Many Requests (with Retry-After) once a caller exceeds its rate. Verified green with go build, go vet, go test, and go test -race on go1.25.3 (module pinned to go 1.23).

maintain.request: Surface rate-limit budget to clients: have the middleware emit standard X-RateLimit-Limit and X-RateLimit-Remaining response headers on every response (success and 429), and add a Limiter.Remaining(r) method that returns the whole tokens left for a request's key without consuming one.

review.bug: In AllowN the availability check uses a strict greater-than (`b.tokens > need`) instead of `>=`, an off-by-one that wrongly denies a request that would exactly exhaust the available tokens (e.g. AllowN(5) on a full bucket of capacity 5 returns false).
